frame_analyses.description: Trainee examining the ligating loop on the table before manipulation. Loop with pusher visible, foam appendage model visible on paper pad. Timing begins.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good initial setup orientation; trainee familiarizing himself with equipment.

frame_analyses.description: Trainee grasping the ligating loop with pusher in both hands. Loop oriented vertically; examining the loop geometry before deployment.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Deliberate handling; good control of the pre-tied loop construct.

frame_analyses.description: Loop being brought down toward the foam appendage. Trainee positioning the loop opening to encircle the target middle appendage marked on the foam model.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Careful alignment; loop opening is being guided over the target appendage.

frame_analyses.description: Loop fully encircles the middle appendage. Trainee holding the loop in position around the appendage base near the marked site. Loop is open and secured around the tissue.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good positioning; loop appears centered on the target appendage at approximately the marked level.

frame_analyses.description: Trainee breaking the pusher at the scored line. Both hands visible working to fracture the plastic pusher outside the trainer boundary.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean break technique; pusher being snapped at the designated scored mark.

frame_analyses.description: Pusher advancing down the loop material. Loop knot cinching tighter around the appendage. Trainee applying steady downward pressure on the pusher.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Smooth advancement; knot is progressively securing around the appendage.

frame_analyses.description: Pusher near its final position. Knot is cinched at what appears to be the marked line on the appendage base. Loop material still extends distally.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Loop appears to be securing at or very near the target mark. Good tension control.

frame_analyses.description: Close-up view of the secured loop. Knot cinched around the appendage; loop position appears aligned with the marked site. Tail of loop material still visible and requires cutting.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Loop is secure and positioned appropriately. Ready for tail cut.

frame_analyses.description: Trainee positioning scissors or cutting instrument to cut the loop tail inside the trainer boundary. Tail is accessible and ready for cutting.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good positioning of cutting instrument; trainee preparing for final cut.

frame_analyses.description: Loop tail being cut with scissors. Cutting occurring inside the trainer boundary. Tail is being severed cleanly.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean cut execution; loop material tail is being removed.

frame_analyses.description: Final view of the completed task. Loop is secured around the appendage at the marked line. Loop tail has been cut. Appendage remains intact and secured.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Task appears complete. Loop is tightly cinched at the appropriate level. No visible tissue damage.

frame_analyses.description: Close-up final inspection. Loop knot is visible, tightly secured around the appendage. No slippage evident. Tail has been removed. Task completion confirmed.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Excellent final positioning. Loop is secure and at target mark.

penalties.description: Loop knot position appears approximately 1-2mm proximal to the marked line on the appendage base. Deviation is minimal but visible in frames 7-8.

loop_security.notes: Loop is tightly cinched and does not appear to slip during or after advancement.

mark_alignment.notes: Loop knot is positioned slightly proximal (toward the base) of the marked line. Deviation is subtle and within acceptable clinical range but visible in close-up frames.

appendage_integrity.notes: Foam appendage remains intact. No destructive compression or tearing evident.

pusher_break.notes: Pusher was cleanly fractured at the designated scored line as required.

tail_cut.notes: Loop tail was cut inside the trainer boundary with scissors. Cut is complete.

confidence_rationale: Completion time is well-observed and unambiguous at 39 seconds. Loop positioning and final state are clearly visible. Mark deviation is estimated from close-up frames but angle of appendage base introduces slight uncertainty; confidence is 0.62 for the 1.5mm penalty, overall confidence 0.78 due to clear task completion and security assessment.
cannot_determine: Exact millimeter deviation from mark — camera angle to appendage base is slightly oblique; the 1-2mm estimate is conservative.
technique_summary: Competent performance with clean technique. Trainee executed the task in 39 seconds, well under the proficiency benchmark of 53 seconds, indicating expert-level speed. Loop positioning was deliberate and accurate; pusher break and advancement were smooth. Minor mark deviation (≤2mm) is clinically acceptable. All task completion requirements were met: loop placed, pusher broken at mark, knot secured, and tail cut inside trainer. No tissue damage or loop slippage.
strengths: Rapid task completion (39s vs. 53s benchmark) demonstrates expert-level efficiency
Smooth pusher advancement with appropriate cinching tension
Clean tail cut with scissors inside trainer boundary
Careful deliberate positioning of loop before advancement
improvement_suggestions: Fine-tune mark alignment to center the knot precisely at the marked line (currently 1-2mm proximal)
video_quality_notes: Clear overhead and close-up views throughout. Equipment is well-lit and visible. Final state confirmation is explicit.
